author: mark
text:
review: empty squash yields delivered_in=None, skip half-push warning

When a claim->review->close cycle produces no code changes (gate tasks,
doc work already on main, bugfixes that became no-ops after merging
main in), delivered_in used to be stamped with the current main HEAD —
an unrelated commit — and detect_half_push flagged every such close
as a false-positive half-push.

Fix: skip the main commit when `git merge --squash` staged nothing,
set delivered_in=None, and mark the state-branch subject
`state: review bl-xxxx no-code` so half-push detection excludes them.
